channel.title: Comments for page "Lord Blackwood and the Big-Foot"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Lord Blackwood and the Big-Foot"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the benefit of tiget2013 and any other YouTube people;

I am Smapti Smaptison and this tale is my composition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Vulpes ex machina…

Also, if you've going to footnote and translate measurements do it for all of them. HE may not be the Foundation, but WE are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added "blackwood" tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and one period where a crow usurped the throne and the rightful prince had to win back his kingdom

Can I upvote posts? No?

*sadface*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Foxes usually don't live longer than 10 years at most (and many are lucky to reach 4), so I have a feeling there's been quite a few more kings than that between Alaric V and Eugenio II.

I don't have a hard backstory or anything, but I sort of imagine that in the 150 years between there was at least one civil war, three succession crises, and one period where a crow usurped the throne and the rightful prince had to win back his kingdom.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly did not see the ending coming, but I am so very pleased that King Alaric V showed up. Blackwood is now the best of all things. ALL THINGS.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have single-handedly made Lord Blackwood my favorite SCP when it comes to total backstory and tale-potential. Kudos, good man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That title.

Oh god, I can't wait.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My headcanon is that he was turned into a sea slug at some point, and that it's just a minor inconvenience for him at the moment. On the other hand, it's certainly possible that he was one all along, or that he's some kind of were-slug who can change form.

I think there's a lot of potential for Lord Blackwood to cross over with other SCPs that could have existed in the Victorian era. I have an idea for a sequel, which I'm tentatively calling "Lord Blackwood and the Great Tarrasque Hunt of '73", which I might write sometime soon after it's percolated in my head for awhile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Implying that he was not a sea slug to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something tells me Lord Blackwood has far more stories in him first…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was good. Very good, But now I want to know WHEN he became a sea slug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Delightful. Just delightful. :-D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … and THAT, ladies and gents, is how you do a good cross between two SCPs. Nice (up)boat'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe you tied this into the damn Redwall SCP, and so well too! Lord Blackwood is my new favorite SCP, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This seems likes it belongs in the wanderer's library interment of tone but the scp connection links it this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. Upvoted hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think someone should draw a lineage from King Alaric the Fifth to the current King Eugenio the Second. It'd be about 6-10 different foxes as Lords and Kings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…sure, let's go with that.

I'm a local myself, and I don't have a specific place in mind for where exactly the "savages" or the Sasquatch were located. This tale was sort of inspired by my driving to Seattle and back from Olympia today, and I chose the Nisqually because I cross over it as part of my daily commute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heading for Tahoma from the village of Seattle, and they went by way of the Nisqually River? His guide was taking him by quite a circuitous route; I suppose that meant the Sasquatch presence was either on the south side of the mountain, or in fact just east of the peak, somewhere around the root of the Nisqually or Cowlitz glacier…

(Sorry, I'm a local, and can't help but wonder why they took him this way. At first I thought they might be headed for Mt. St. Helens, but then it would have made more sense to start from the fort at Olympia.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, crazy coincidence- my SCP-1646 was probably active near Mount Rainier in 1857- it was being studied by the Foundation as early as 1863.

EDIT: I do like this tale, certainly. You work fast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So that's why the name sounded familiar… still, I wouldn't complain if were a crossover.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: im pleasantly surprised, i was afraid this was going to be a "Don't be afraid of the dark" crossover, since thats the only lord blackwood i know, this is much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Smapti you devil. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh hell yes.

Hell yes.

You sir, are a Gentleman and a Scholar.

All the upvotes of fabled Xanadu pale in comparison to what I wish I could give this story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent! More proof that even kind of silly ideas (a Christian fox, an proper English sea slug, Bigfoot) can be made into something wonderful in the hands of a good writer. Congratulations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As soon as I read the word "Sasquatch," I was pretty sure SCP-1000 was involved. I was pleasantly surprised.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even though Smapti beat me to this idea, I shall not give up. Lord Blackwood shall return in…

Lord Blackwood and the Flame Pits of the Orient!

Featuring Lord Blackwood's arch-nemesis…

Lord William Barnaby Smithington III!

Stay tuned as soon as I clear out my backlog!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I now present a tale based on the new and popular SCP-1867, featuring a crossover with one of my own SCPs which someone in the comments thread said was bound to happen.
